sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Comfort Systems USA Raises Quarterly Dividend by $0.10
feed.summary: Comfort Systems USA (NYSE: FIX) announced that its board of directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.90 per share, marking a $0.10 increase from its previous dividend. This dividend is payable on August 24, 2026, to stockholders of record as of August 13, 2026. The company, a leading provider of HVAC and electrical contracting services, operates 206 locations across 150 cities in the U.S.

feed.title: Former Stifel Indie Team Managing $1 Billion Jumps to Raymond James
feed.summary: KWM Wealth Advisory, a former Stifel Independent Advisors team managing $1 billion in client assets, has moved to Raymond James Financial Services. This move follows Stifel's sale of its independent unit to Equitable Advisors in April. The team, led by Kenneth A. Sanchez, Lee S. Wolfe, Mitchell E. Kauffman, and KaNoi Lam, will operate out of Pasadena, California, and Honolulu, Hawaii, and had previously joined Stifel from Wells Fargo in 2022.

feed.title: L3Harris Announces Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.25 per common share. The dividend is payable on September 18, 2026, to shareholders of record as of September 4, 2026. L3Harris is described as a "Trusted Disruptor in defense tech," providing end-to-end technology solutions across various domains for national security.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated Declares Quarterly Cash Dividends On Its Common and Preferred Stocks
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated announced that its Board of Directors has declared quarterly cash dividends on both its common stock and six series of preferred stock. The common stock dividend remains unchanged at $0.155 per share and is payable on October 1, 2026. Dividends for the preferred stocks are also scheduled, with most payable on October 15, 2026, and one series on November 20, 2026.

feed.title: Allegion plc SEC Filing (Jul 23, 2026)
feed.summary: This article details an SEC Form 144 filing by Allegion plc (ALLE) on July 23, 2026. The filing indicates a proposed sale of 687 ordinary shares acquired through the exercise of stock options, with an aggregate market value of $106,485. The sale is to be handled by UBS Financial Services Inc. and is scheduled for the same date on the NYSE.

feed.title: ‘AI Won’t Replace FAs, But Advisors Using AI Will’: Raymond James CEO Says
feed.summary: Raymond James CEO Paul Shoukry emphasizes the firm's strategic investment in AI to enhance financial advisors' capabilities and client relationships, stating that "AI won’t replace advisors, but advisors who use AI will replace advisors who do not use AI." The firm launched its proprietary AI agent, RAImond, and an AI Academy, which have seen high adoption and satisfaction rates. These AI initiatives are part of a larger $1.1 billion annual technology investment, primarily aimed at the Private Client Group, and are expected to yield significant long-term ROI despite initial integration costs.

feed.title: Intel Forecast Shatters Estimates, Fueled by Data Center Growth
feed.summary: Intel Corp. has issued a surprisingly strong revenue forecast for the current period, exceeding analyst estimates. The company anticipates sales between $15.8 billion and $16.8 billion for the third quarter. This positive outlook is attributed to a surge in data center spending, which is helping to drive the chipmaker's turnaround.

feed.title: Epcoritamab U.S. Lymphoma Trial Does Not Meet Survival Endpoint
feed.summary: Genmab and AbbVie announced that their Phase 3 EPCORE® DLBCL-1 trial, evaluating epcoritamab for relapsed/refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), did not meet its primary endpoint of overall survival in the U.S. market. While epcoritamab is already approved under accelerated pathways for similar conditions, this news indicates that the trial failed to show a statistically significant improvement in overall survival when compared to chemoimmunotherapy. The companies continue to evaluate epcoritamab's potential across various clinical programs and geographies.

feed.title: Airlines fight headwinds: Southwest could cut more jobs, American reports double-digit profit decline
feed.summary: North Texas airlines are facing economic headwinds due to rising fuel costs and geopolitical uncertainty. Southwest Airlines is considering additional layoffs as part of cost-cutting measures, while American Airlines reported an 88% drop in quarterly profit despite achieving record revenue.

feed.title: Newmont maintains 9.2% stake in Metallic
feed.summary: Newmont Corp. has invested C$902,916 to maintain its approximately 9.2% ownership in Metallic Minerals Corp. This investment will support Metallic's exploration and development projects in Yukon and Colorado, including the Keno Silver project, which has planned 5,000 meters of drilling in 2026, and the La Plata project. Metallic is advancing these projects with a focus on expanding existing resources and testing new targets for silver, copper, and other critical minerals.

feed.title: Nearly 1.6M Dozen Eggs Sold at Kroger and Other Stores Recalled due to Potential Salmonella Contamination
feed.summary: Midwest Poultry Services has recalled nearly 1.6 million dozen eggs due to potential Salmonella contamination identified at its Texas farms. The affected eggs were sold at Kroger and Brookshire Grocery stores, and other smaller retailers, across multiple states including Texas, Louisiana, Oklahoma, Arkansas, New Mexico, and Mississippi, with sell-by dates ranging from July 20 to August 17, 2026. Consumers are advised to return the eggs for a refund, and while the company has taken precautionary measures and launched an investigation, no illnesses have been linked to the recall so far.

feed.title: OpenAI Commits to Cover Costs for Planned $20B Georgia Project
feed.summary: Southern Company (NYSE: SO) has joined President Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge, emphasizing its commitment to customer-first growth in AI and advanced technologies by ensuring rate stability and reliability. As part of this, OpenAI's planned $20 billion project in Effingham County, Georgia, will cover its full infrastructure and electric service costs and provide up to 1,000 megawatts of flexible demand response to support grid reliability and long-term customer savings. This approach, which includes multiyear base rate freezes for Georgia Power and Alabama Power, aims to protect existing customers from the costs associated with new energy-intensive demand.

feed.title: Synopsys outlook raised to positive by S&P on debt reduction
feed.summary: S&P Global Ratings has revised its outlook on Synopsys Inc. to positive from stable, while affirming its BBB rating, due to the company's significant debt reduction. Synopsys repaid $4.3 billion of the debt incurred from its Ansys acquisition and used proceeds from Nvidia's $2 billion investment for further reduction, aiming for a net leverage target below 1x. S&P expects Synopsys's revenue growth to improve, driven by better pipeline execution and a strategic shift towards high-value engagements in AI silicon, potentially leading to a rating upgrade within 12 to 24 months.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $1.25
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.25 per share, maintaining its previous payout and offering a forward yield of 1.69%. The company, a leader in the Aerospace & Defense industry with a market cap of $55.33 billion and a GF Score of 87/100, aims to return value to shareholders through consistent dividends. Despite insider selling and an average financial strength rating, its strong growth potential makes it a mixed but interesting prospect for investors.

feed.title: S&P Global downgrades Lennar outlook on margin pressures By Investing.com
feed.summary: S&P Global Ratings has downgraded Lennar Corp.'s outlook from positive to stable, while affirming its BBB issuer credit rating. This revision is attributed to declining revenue and compressed margins faced by the homebuilder, stemming from affordability issues and higher mortgage rates. Lennar's revenue dropped 8% and gross profit margins fell to 15.4% for the six months ended May 31, 2026, with expectations for continued margin pressures in the near term.

feed.title: S&P Global downgrades Lennar outlook on margin pressures
feed.summary: S&P Global Ratings revised its outlook on Lennar Corp. from positive to stable while affirming all ratings, including the BBB issuer credit rating. This downgrade is attributed to declining revenue and compressed margins due to a cyclical downturn, affordability constraints, and higher mortgage rates. The ratings firm anticipates continued margin pressures in the near term, with EBITDA margins expected to normalize in 2027 and 2028.

feed.title: Ameren, Duke Energy upgraded; Entergy, NiSource poised for data center load growth, KeyBanc says
feed.summary: KeyBanc has upgraded Ameren (AEE) to Overweight, citing increasing visibility for load growth and potential earnings revisions. Duke Energy (DUK) was also upgraded to Overweight due to its defensive nature, undemanding valuation, and anticipated growth from large load customers in the U.S. Southeast. KeyBanc further highlighted Entergy (ETR) and NiSource (NI) as well-positioned to capitalize on data center load growth by effectively balancing growth opportunities with affordability and political considerations.

feed.title: Change Management Meets Enterprise Reality
feed.summary: This article argues that continuous change has become the norm in enterprise environments, necessitating a shift from episodic transformation to ongoing conversations and adaptability. Organizations that successfully navigate sustained change prioritize transparent communication, build trust through consistent information sharing, and foster buy-in by detailing progress, future steps, and impact on employees. The goal is to move beyond a "change happening to us" mindset to one where employees are actively involved, accelerating the pace of transformation required by modern business demands.

feed.title: Crown Castle (CCI) Completes $1 Billion Buyback Of More Than 11 Million Shares
feed.summary: Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) has completed a $1 billion share buyback, repurchasing over 11 million shares, which accounts for 2.59% of its share count. This buyback comes as the company's stock has declined significantly over the past one and five years. The repurchase aims to concentrate future earnings and dividends across a smaller share base and could impact per-share metrics like EPS and the P/E ratio, especially since the stock currently trades below analyst and fair value estimates.

feed.title: Abbott Stock And 2 Dividend Ideas For Retirement Income
feed.summary: This article explores three dividend stocks—Abbott Laboratories (ABT), Yü Group (AIM:YU.), and Artesian Resources (ARTN.A)—as potential retirement income sources, especially in light of Social Security reform concerns. It analyzes each company's overview, operations, market cap, and investment considerations, highlighting their strengths and risks for income-focused investors. The piece suggests these stocks could help diversify retirement portfolios and mitigate reliance on government programs.

feed.title: Form 8-K FirstCash Holdings, Inc. For: Jul 23
feed.summary: FirstCash Holdings, Inc. filed a Form 8-K announcing its financial results for the three and six months ended June 30, 2026. The company also declared a third-quarter cash dividend of $0.42 per common share and authorized $150 million in share repurchases. The share repurchase program has no set completion time and allows for various purchase methods.

feed.title: Arista Networks Stock Runs On A Different Fuel
feed.summary: Arista Networks (ANET) is highlighted as a standout stock due to its unique return stream and low correlation to the S&P 500, making it a compelling growth engine despite its amplified volatility. The company is experiencing strong demand for AI infrastructure, leading to a raised revenue forecast, but faces supply chain challenges causing gross margin pressure. Investors are advised to focus on how Arista manages the gap between demand and supply rather than just daily price movements, emphasizing the importance of diversified portfolios with stocks that exhibit independent performance.

feed.title: US BANCORP DE SEC Filing (Jul 23, 2026)
feed.summary: This article details a Form 144 SEC filing by US Bancorp DE (USB) on July 23, 2026. The filing indicates a proposed sale of 22,968 shares of common stock with an aggregate market value of $1,453,415.04. The shares were acquired through a stock option exercise on the same date and are to be sold via Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC on the NYSE.

feed.title: KB Financial Group Inc Stock 12‑Month Price Target Raised to $140.29, Implies 18% Upside
feed.summary: The average 12-month price target for KB Financial Group Inc stock has been raised from $138.51 to $140.29 by 22 analysts, implying an 18% potential upside from its Jul. 22 closing price. The consensus rating among these analysts remains a "Buy," with no "Holds" or "Sells."

feed.title: PPL tariffs put new grid costs on the customers creating demand
feed.summary: PPL Corporation has signed the White House's Ratepayer Protection Pledge, aiming to ensure that data centers bear the costs of the energy and infrastructure they demand, protecting existing customers. The company highlighted that many of these principles are already integrated into their regulator-approved tariffs in Pennsylvania and Kentucky. PPL also emphasized its efforts in grid reliability, planning, engineering studies, and a joint venture with Blackstone Infrastructure to build new generation resources for growing data center demand.

feed.title: Applovin Corp Stock (APP) Moved Down by 3.58% on Jul 23: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: AppLovin Corp (APP) stock declined by 3.58% on July 23, driven by broader weakness in the technology sector, macroeconomic uncertainty, and concerns about mobile gaming market saturation. Technical indicators show a sell signal, and the stock is experiencing profit-taking. Despite analysts' "Buy" ratings and high price targets, the company faces risks related to extreme valuation, sustainability of software platform growth, revenue concentration in mobile gaming, and regulatory challenges in digital privacy.

feed.title: Danaher Corp Stock (DHR) Moved Up by 6.78% on Jul 23: What Signal Does It Send?
feed.summary: Danaher Corp (DHR) stock rose 6.78% due to strong quarterly results, exceeding analyst expectations, and indicating a recovery in bioprocessing demand. Management raised full-year earnings and revenue guidance, reflecting confidence in sustainable growth across its biotechnology and life sciences segments. Broader market dynamics and institutional investor rotation into high-quality companies also contributed to the positive momentum.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Inc Stock (BKNG) Moved Down by 3.16% on Jul 23: What Investors Need To Know
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) shares fell by 3.16% on July 23 due to a combination of softening consumer discretionary travel demand and increased regulatory scrutiny under Europe's Digital Markets Act. Institutional investors are reducing their exposure to the stock amid concerns about cooling regional market growth and pre-earnings positioning, with analysts suggesting potential tempered forward guidance. The stock's technical indicators also suggest neutral to sell conditions, while media coverage remains moderate with an overall bullish market sentiment for the sector.

feed.title: Lockheed Martin Corp Stock (LMT) Moved Up by 10.96% on Jul 23: A Full Analysis
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin Corp's stock (LMT) surged 10.96% on July 23rd due to strong second-quarter earnings that beat estimates, driven by growth in its Missiles and Fire Control segment and F-35 deliveries. The company also raised its full-year guidance, citing a record order backlog and high international defense demand, which was further boosted by analyst upgrades. However, technical indicators suggest caution, and the article notes specific risks such as F-35 software delays and procurement funding uncertainty.

feed.title: Frequency Electronics Reports Increased Demand From Proliferated Satellite Programs
feed.summary: Frequency Electronics has announced $18 million in new contract wins, including an $11 million award from a major aerospace prime contractor for a proliferated satellite program. This marks the company's second significant contract for such programs this year, and President and CEO Tom McClelland anticipates further wins in this high-margin area. The company also reported a record funded backlog of $111 million, a nearly 60% year-over-year increase, following investments in engineering talent to prepare for future production growth.

feed.title: PPL Corporation signs Ratepayer Protection Pledge and highlights existing customer protections in Pennsylvania and Kentucky
feed.summary: PPL Corporation has signed the White House's Ratepayer Protection Pledge, emphasizing its commitment to affordability, reliability, and responsible growth while protecting existing customers from the rising energy demands of data centers. The company highlights that its utilities in Pennsylvania and Kentucky already have regulator-approved tariffs and rate structures designed to ensure large energy users pay their fair share of infrastructure costs. PPL aims to support economic growth and data center expansion in a way that maintains grid reliability and affordability for all customers.

feed.title: B2B Marketing Has A Purpose Problem
feed.summary: Many B2B marketing leaders feel a disconnect between their perception of marketing as a strategic driver of growth and their peers' view of it as a support function. This issue stems from undefined marketing mandates, leading to diffused priorities and a constraint on growth. Forrester's B2B Marketing Purpose Model offers four zones—Supporting, Promoting, Partnering, Driving—to help organizations explicitly define marketing's role, requiring C-suite alignment to foster clarity, accountability, and greater business impact.

feed.title: EXEL Maintained by Morgan Stanley -- Price Target Raised to $53
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained an Equal-Weight rating on Exelixis (EXEL) while raising its price target from $50.00 to $53.00, an increase of 6%. Despite this cautious optimism, GuruFocus assesses EXEL as 29.1% overvalued with a GF Value™ of $42.86 against its current price of $55.34. The company holds a strong GF Score™ of 94/100, driven by high profitability and growth, though recent insider selling activity of $9.7 million may warrant investor caution.

feed.title: United Rentals, Waste Connections win analyst praise after earnings beats (WCN:NYSE)
feed.summary: United Rentals (URI) and Waste Connections (WCN) received analyst praise after exceeding quarterly earnings expectations and raising their full-year outlooks. Conversely, Rollins (ROL) faced new scrutiny following another disappointing quarter. BNP Paribas noted that investors would likely reward URI and WCN for their strong performance and positive outlooks.

feed.title: INCY Maintained by Morgan Stanley -- Price Target Raised to $104.00
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has maintained its Equal-Weight rating for Incyte Corporation (INCY) and raised its price target to $104.00 from $103.00. Despite this slight increase, GuruFocus indicates that INCY is currently overvalued by 15.8% with a GF Value™ of $100.11 against a trading price of $115.96. The company boasts a strong GF Score™ of 87/100, reflecting robust financial strength, profitability, and momentum, yet insider selling and the overvaluation suggest caution for investors.

feed.title: Wendell Weeks, Kim Frock handed keys to Corning, 'permanent open doors'
feed.summary: Corning City Mayor Pam Walker presented commemorative glass "Keys to the City" to Wendell Weeks, CEO of Corning Inc., and Kim Frock, founder of the Alternative School for Math and Science, during the company's 175th Anniversary Celebration. The award recognizes their deep connection to the region and Corning Inc.'s long legacy. The mayor highlighted their significant contributions to the city, symbolizing the community's gratitude and permanent open doors.

feed.title: Ford plans to use Apple Maps in its next-gen EVs (F:NYSE)
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company announced on Wednesday that Apple Maps will be deeply integrated into its next generation of electric vehicles, while Apple CarPlay will remain available. The automaker's upcoming Universal Electric Vehicle platform is designed to incorporate Apple Maps, signaling a closer partnership with Apple for its EV technology. This move suggests Ford is aiming to enhance the in-car navigation experience for its future EV owners.

feed.title: EWBC Maintained by DA Davidson -- Price Target Raised to $155
feed.summary: DA Davidson has maintained a "Buy" rating for East West Bancorp (EWBC) and raised its price target from $150.00 to $155.00, indicating a positive outlook for the company. Despite this, GuruFocus assesses EWBC as 26.4% overvalued with a GF Value™ of $102.82 against a current price of $129.91, and notes significant insider selling of $10.5 million in shares over the last three months. The stock's GF Score™ is 84/100, highlighting strong growth and momentum but a low financial strength rating.

feed.title: The Sher sisters share a charge in the energy industry
feed.summary: Sisters Lauren and Rebecca Sher, originally from Bay City, Texas, have both forged successful careers within the energy industry at NextEra Energy Resources despite taking different paths. Lauren works in Origination, forming partnerships for energy needs, while Rebecca is in a development role bringing energy solutions to communities. Their shared values of hard work and commitment to service, instilled from childhood, continue to guide them in their professional and personal lives.

feed.title: Why Is Walt Disney Stock Falling Thursday?
feed.summary: Walt Disney Co. (NYSE: DIS) stock is falling due to a European patent ruling against it, renewed calls from investors for corporate restructuring, and regulatory uncertainties concerning its media business. Technical analysis shows the stock is in a longer-term downtrend, trading below various moving averages, and is near its 52-week low. Despite this, analysts maintain a consensus Buy rating with an average price forecast of $125.42, and Disney is scheduled to report second-quarter earnings on August 5th.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) Given New $38.00 Price Target at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has raised its price target for Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) to $38.00 from $33.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating, which suggests a potential 27.88% upside. Other analysts like Citigroup and Barclays have also increased their price targets, though the overall consensus remains a "Hold" with an average target of $33.40. KDP shares saw a slight dip of 1.6% to $29.71 despite beating EPS estimates in its last quarterly report, albeit with revenue below expectations.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Insulet Corporation (PODD) Shareholders Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP reminds investors of an upcoming August 31, 2026 deadline to file a lead plaintiff motion in a class action lawsuit against Insulet Corporation (PODD). The lawsuit alleges that Insulet made materially false and misleading statements and failed to disclose manufacturing issues with its Omnipod products, leading to significant stock price drops in March and May 2026. Investors who suffered losses between February 21, 2025, and May 26, 2026, are urged to contact the firm.

feed.title: AMD expects compute market to reach $2T by 2030 as Helios enters full production
feed.summary: AMD officially launched its Helios rack-scale system, which is now in full production, at its Advancing AI 2026 event. The company anticipates the AI accelerator total addressable market will reach $1.4 trillion by 2030, with GPUs forming the bulk of this, while the broader compute market is expected to hit at least $2 trillion by 2030 due to expanding AI adoption. AMD, with its diverse product portfolio and strategic partnerships, believes it is well-positioned in the competitive AI hardware market to meet various customer needs.

feed.title: Fortinet heads into earnings with product growth in the spotlight
feed.summary: Fortinet Inc. (NASDAQ:FTNT) is gearing up to release its second-quarter results on July 29, with analysts from Jefferies emphasizing the necessity for accelerated product revenue growth to bolster investor confidence. The firm anticipates strong product trends driven by price increases and improved channel checks, but investors are keen to see if this momentum can be sustained into 2027 and if subscription services remain robust. Billings and operating margins are also under scrutiny, with Jefferies expecting Fortinet to exceed billings guidance for the quarter while keeping an eye on rising memory costs impacting future margins.

feed.title: Garmin Pilot adds Mobile Clearance Delivery for streamlined IFR clearances
feed.summary: Garmin has introduced Mobile Clearance Delivery for its Garmin Pilot app, allowing Premium subscribers on iOS devices to obtain and cancel IFR clearances electronically at select U.S. airports. This new feature, developed in collaboration with the FAA and MITRE, aims to reduce radio communications, minimize errors, and improve efficiency for pilots and air traffic control. The phased rollout is expected to expand nationwide through 2028, with five airports currently participating in testing and evaluation.

feed.title: Kroger plans to shift jobs to new capability center in India, analyst says
feed.summary: Kroger Co. is reportedly establishing a new capability center in India to offshore numerous job functions. An industry analyst indicates this move is intended to save the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

feed.title: What's Going On With Snap-on Stock on Thursday?
feed.summary: Snap-on (SNA) shares reacted to their second-quarter 2026 earnings report, which exceeded analyst expectations for diluted EPS and net sales, driven by organic growth and improved gross margins. Despite the initial positive reaction, the stock later gave up its gains, trading down 1.94% by the time of publication on Thursday. The company reported increased net earnings, strong performance in Commercial & Industrial sales, and strategic acquisitions, while also detailing cash flow and a positive outlook from its CEO amidst a "turbulent" environment.

feed.title: T-Mobile's Growth Is Slowing as Rivals Push Back
feed.summary: T-Mobile's growth is anticipated to slow as competitors AT&T and Verizon are expected to catch up, leading to a stabilization of market share among the three major carriers. Despite current high competition, a rational competitive landscape is foreseen, which should result in stable pricing, disciplined investment, and expanding cash flow. The article emphasizes T-Mobile's strong brand, network, and spectrum as drivers for near-term market share gains.

feed.title: Union Pacific's Intermodal Volume Rebounding Sharply This Year
feed.summary: Union Pacific (UNP) is experiencing a sharp rebound in its intermodal volume this year. The company implemented precision scheduled railroading (PSR) in late 2018, a strategy that has proved effective in improving network efficiency and increasing incremental margins. This report is an analyst note discussing Union Pacific's performance.

feed.title: Travelers sues rival insurer over strings-attached coverage acceptance
feed.summary: Travelers has sued Mesa Underwriters Specialty Insurance Company (MESA) in federal court, seeking a declaratory judgment that MESA must fully defend and indemnify two of Travelers' insureds in a construction accident claim. Travelers argues that MESA, as the primary insurer, should cover the claim from a demolition worker's injury. The core of the dispute lies in MESA's conditional acceptance of the tender, which Travelers contends only committed to defense without indemnity and refused to repay Travelers' prior defense costs.
